Commissioner ]. r. O'Brien won
his fight against the Dennis ware
house scheme to build the pro-
posed new E. Sprague trestle when
Tuesday night the council con-
Cured in the recommendations OF
the board of works that plans and
specifications for the work be made
and bids called for. G. b. Dennis
scheme was to fill in between con-
crete walls and trestle that portion
of the gully through which the In
land systems trains run. He
wanted the city to pay $4,000 for
the work. The company owns mos,
of the property there which now is
useless on account of the cliffs.
Dennis proposed to use this rock,
and by having the city pay $4,000
net a huge profit, as it would other
wise cost Dennis nearly 100000 to
have the crock removed. Thus he
would be getting the city to pay
for the improvement 10 MS COM
pany's property, as well as for the
trestle and fill. After careful fig
uring with City Engineer Mcintyre
Commissioner O'Brien reached the
conclusion that such a job as Den
mis- proposed should not cost more
than $4,000, instead of $4,000.
Just at present the work cannot
be taken up by day labor on &C.
count of the financial conditions,
but probably by the time a contrac-
tor has completed the job condi-
lions will have improved. 1t Is pro-
posed to charge the Dennis COM
pany for the removal of it's rock,
as we'll as force a partial payment
from both the Inland and Washing
ton Water Power companies, to be
equally benefited with the city by
the new trestle, Corporation Coun-
sci Hamblen has submitted an opin-
ion In which he says the city can
force these payments.
